Arc Flash Study

Contact Us

What is it?

Power quality analysis checks the power supply for safety and efficiency.

Years of experience?

LETS DISCOVER MORE

CareLabs is a cost-effective provider of electrical arc flash analysis, investigation, and certification services for organizations. Skilled professionals will evaluate arc flash threats, personnel training, documentation, and PPE (Personal Protective Equipment) needs to ensure that your electrical safety program is current, compliant, and thorough. CareLabs can identify arc flash concerns in a timely manner and take the necessary steps to reduce the impact of harmonic distortions to ensure the equipment’s safety.c flashes are analyzed and investigated via ETAP (Electrical Transient Analysis Program) software. An arc flash is the amount of light and heat produced by an arc fault. This occurs when current travels via an air gap between two conductors. 

The arc flash ionizes the surrounding air and emits a substantial amount of light and heat (approximately 35,000 degrees Fahrenheit). An arc flash analysis is the investigation and examination of a facility’s electrical systems to determine the incident energy available for each piece of electrical equipment. Arc flash studies promote safe working conditions by developing safety standards for skilled electricians working on equipment and circuits. Also included are updated one-line electrical schematics and equipment locations, and a short circuit and coordination analysis. 

Research Goals for Arc Flash:

  • Identify the magnitude of the damage caused by the incident energy emitted during an arc flash fault.
  • Altering the configurations of protective equipment to limit exposure to incident energy
  • Develop safety guidelines for arc flash risks.
  • Observe the rules and laws that apply in your nation.
  • Identify the type of PPE utilized on the project.

The negative effects of arc flash:

  • Arcs cause serious burns to the skin, face, and eyes.
  • Inhaled gases and heated particles can cause serious burns to the trachea and lungs.
  • Injuries resulting from flying debris and loosened parts

Arc flash studies are vital for reducing hazards and enhancing the safety of electrical staff, as well as the business’s overall safety. Studies of arc flash contribute to the determination of electrical danger levels and the installation of appropriate safety measures to reduce the risk of burns and accidents Arc flash analysis is necessary for all utilities, manufacturing, industrial, and commercial companies that use motor control, panel boards, switchboards, and electrical disconnects.  

Benefits of Arc Flash Research:

  • Improved human and machine safety
  • Reduced medical and legal costs
  • Compliance with safety regulatory requirements
  • A reduction in the amount of code breaches and fines

The following processes are implemented for arc flash analysis:

  • Data collecting
  • Conducting site inspections
  • Design of a system
  • Examination of arc flashes

Work Method of CareLabs Arc Flash Research:

CareLabs provides superior services and will provide any services needed by the construction crew, local contractor, or engineering firm.

Our work process consists of the following steps:

  • Data collecting
  • ETAP modelling of an electrical power system
  • Examination of the short-circuit
  • A study on coordination of overcurrent protection devices
  • An inquiry into arc flashes
  • Conducting arc flash testing
  • Keeping a record

Methods to reduce arc flash hazard:

  • Full-size one-line diagrams
  • Posting arc flash markings
  • The completed report

Advantages of Using CareLabs' Services:

  • Develop electrical systems that are more secure and compliant with rules and standards.
  • Integrated modules for comprehensive short circuit, overcurrent coordination, device assessment, and arc flash evaluation are integrated with bus ratings and protection devices.
  • Making the workplace more secure
  • Rapid and effortless alternative design suggestion
  • Enhanced margins of safety through user-defined arc fault tolerances
  • Save time by utilizing our computer-aided analysis to produce arc flash labels automatically.
  • Reduce losses in productivity and maintenance expenses.

Crew members are strategically positioned across CareLabs to guarantee that our specialists are readily available in the event of a routine or emergency scenario. CareLabs has become an ISO 9001:2008-accredited business with excellent customer feedback very rapidly. We provide arc flash investigation and analysis services in all major cities, including Taipei, Kaohsiung, Taichung, and Tainan. Contact us immediately for a free quote on arc flash analysis and study. 

RELATED SERVICES

Arc Flash Study Arc flash analyses, also known as arc flash hazard analyses, are the process of looking at electrical systems to identify potential dangers related to arc flash accidents.
Read 01 05 Harmonic Study Harmonic voltage analysis is a type of mathematical method that is used to estimate potential resonances and harmonic distortion levels based on the data that has been collected.


Read 02 05 Power Quality Study Power quality analysis is the process of assessing the electrical power supply to ensure that it satisfies the requirements necessary for the safe and efficient operation of electrical systems.
Read 03 05 Power System Analysis The Power System Study & Analysis service assures the safety, efficacy, and efficiency of your facility's power system in the event that there is an interruption in the supply of electrical power.






Read 04 05 Motor Start Analysis A motor start analysis service may offer insight into the optimal method of starting a motor and the optimal power system design to minimize the impact of the motor starting on the power system.
Read 05 05

Home /Services /Harmonic Study and Analysis

Harmonic Study and
Analysis

Contact Us

What is it?

Power quality analysis checks the power supply for safety and efficiency.

Years of experience?

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Ut elit tellus, luctus

Terms of service?

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Ut elit tellus,

Electrical Standards

RED

LRED

LRED

RED

RED

LRED

LRED

RED

ED

LRED

LETS DISCOVER MORE

CareLabs offers electrical installation services such as safety inspections, testing, calibration, and certification, as well as comprehensive harmonic research and analysis. Professionals will evaluate harmonic hazards, training programs, documentation, and personal protective equipment (PPE) to ensure that your electrical safety program is current, thorough, and compliant. CareLabs can detect harmonics early on and take the necessary steps to limit the impact of harmonic distortions to guarantee the safety of the equipment. Harmonic voltages and currents can result in inefficiency in an electrical system. They generate inefficient heat and might reduce the quality of the power produced. Harmonic voltage analysis is a mathematical approach used to predict potential resonances and degrees of harmonic distortion. 

Harmonic voltage analysis is a mathematical technique for predicting potential resonances and harmonic distortion levels based on empirical evidence. Nonlinear electrical switching equipment, including AC variable frequency drives, DC drives, fluorescent lights, and computers, introduces harmonic frequencies into the electrical system. High voltage/current harmonic levels provide a considerable risk of equipment failure. Consequently, a timely harmonic inquiry and analysis is needed. Currently, there are 5% harmonic restrictions in systems using 415 volts, with 4% odd harmonics and 2% even harmonics.

Harmonic Distortion Has the Following Effects:

  • It causes circulating current and high voltage.
  • Equipment failure and damage owing to significant voltage distortion
  • Fault-initiated triggering of branch circuit breakers
  • Deviation measurement
  • Fires and explosions in the wiring and distribution system
  • Power outages caused by generators
  • A decrease in the system’s power factor
  • Expensive installation and energy costs
  • Productivity losses
  • Downtime and unnecessary vehicle journeys
  • Larger motor size

Harmonic difficulties are extremely uncommon, but when they do occur, they tend to reduce the reliability, quality, and efficiency of the power system. Therefore, harmonic inquiry and analysis is essential. 

  • Protects against voltage and current harmonics
  • Limitation of frequency volatility
  • Voltage and current imbalance
  • Improves power factor analysis and keeps the result near to one.
  • Regulates voltage fluctuations.

Harmonic research and analysis will provide insight into the probable cause and effect of power quality concerns, facilitating the avoidance of harmonic-related problems during the design phase and reducing the likelihood of unexpected effects. 

Workflow of Harmonic Analysis and Research at CareLabs:

  • Emphasize available nonlinear loads, capacitor banks, and long medium-voltage cables on the single-line diagram.
  • Determine which in-plant system buses are susceptible to harmonic distortions
  • Determine the point of common connection (PCC).
  • Collect harmonics data for all nonlinear loads.
  • Acquire crucial information on voltage and current harmonics at the specified PCC, including the maximum and minimum short circuit fault levels
  • Conduct harmonic analysis using ETAP on the electrical network.
  • Compute the global and discrete values for current and voltage wave distortion at the buses and PCC.
  • Draw the harmonic frequency spectrum.
  • A suitable mitigation approach is proposed and implemented if harmonic distortion levels exceed acceptable limits.
  • After including the mitigation solution, rerun the analysis.

CareLabs Service Advantages:

If harmonics in the background have already been measured, the second stage consists of higher ratings.

  • If the background is less than 25%, stage 2 allows variable converter size based on fault level and converter type.
  • If the background is between 25% and 75%, 50% of the allowed converter grade may be used.
  • An exhaustive harmonic analysis is performed on the top background.

CareLabs has crew members stationed in several locations to ensure that our specialists are readily accessible in the event of a routine or urgent circumstance. CareLabs has rapidly established itself as an ISO 9001:2008-accredited organization with a devoted customer that has provided exceptional feedback. In several important Taiwan cities, including Taipei, Kaohsiung, Taichung, and Tainan, we conduct harmonic research and analysis. Contact us right now to organize your harmonic analysis and study in Taiwanese or to obtain a quote! 

RELATED SERVICES

Read 01 05 Harmonic Study Harmonic voltage analysis is a type of mathematical method that is used to estimate potential resonances and harmonic distortion levels based on the data that has been collected.


Read 02 05 Power Quality Study Power quality analysis is the process of assessing the electrical power supply to ensure that it satisfies the requirements necessary for the safe and efficient operation of electrical systems.
Read 03 05 Power System Analysis The Power System Study & Analysis service assures the safety, efficacy, and efficiency of your facility's power system in the event that there is an interruption in the supply of electrical power.






Read 04 05 Motor Start Analysis A motor start analysis service may offer insight into the optimal method of starting a motor and the optimal power system design to minimize the impact of the motor starting on the power system.

Motor Start Analysis

Contact Us

What is it?

Power quality analysis checks the power supply for safety and efficiency.

Years of experience?

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Ut elit tellus, luctus

Terms of service?

Electrical Standards

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LETS DISCOVER MORE

CareLabs provides businesses in Taiwan with a specialized Motor start research and analysis service. The primary purpose of our motor start analysis service is to evaluate the non-typical impacts of starting a large motor. In addition to appropriately connecting the electric motor to the line voltage, there are several other criteria to assess before starting an electric motor, such nuisance tripping, excessive nominal currents, and flickering or fading light, all of which indicate a faulty power supply. CareLabs will assign qualified personnel to your location to collect current and historical motor installation data. Then, using the acquired data and manufacturer input, calculations are performed to forecast future consequences, and recommendations for mitigating the impacts are provided. 

Both static and dynamic simulation approaches may be utilized to evaluate motor starting procedures:

  • The measured starting impedance of the electric motor is modelled to get the solution for the load flow.
  • The short-circuit technique is used in pre-fault short circuit circumstances, whereas voltage drop calculations are used for motor starting currents.
  • The dynamic model of the electric motor may be used to determine the effect if a detailed dynamic model of the electric motor’s circuit and load-torque characteristics is available. of motor starting in a conventional transient stability procedure.
  • When extensive dynamic model information for an electric motor is not available, transient stability simulations can be employed.

Motor Start Analysis Is Required in Taiwanese:

The use of motors in contemporary industrial systems and the size of electric motors are increasing dramatically. When a large motor linked to a line is started, it can disrupt the electric motor, locally connected loads, and electrically distant buses. The terminal voltage should be at or above 80% of the rated voltage when starting an electric motor. The square of a motor’s terminal voltage is exactly proportional to torque; hence voltage difference affects the load torque of a motor. In the event of a substantial voltage drop, running machines may approach their breaking torque and decelerate or halt. Voltage drops influence, among other things, electrical equipment, sensitive control devices, and lighting loads. In addition, a large initial current reduces terminal voltage, which increases the current taken by all systems supplying a continuous load. Additionally, defective low-voltage relays are identified. 

When Is a Motor Start Analysis Necessary?

  • Prior to or during the acquisition of a large motor.
  • If no generator is provided and the motor rating exceeds 30% of the base KVA rating of the supply transformer.
  • If the generator is the sole source of power for the system and the motor rating exceeds 10-15% of the generator’s KVA rating.
  • If the generator is the only source of power for the system. If many motors start concurrently in an industrial setting, a motor start study may be essential.
  • Concerns with voltage loss while starting a motor with a large capacity are exacerbated by the limited capacity of smaller power supply. The examination of motor start-up aids comprehension of the output of systems incorporating minute generators.

Motor Start Benefits Analysis:

Examination of motor launch Utilizing a motor or starting technique with favorable speed-torque characteristics is useful. To avoid exceeding the torque constraints of the equipment, any unexpected loads must be accelerated under supervision. At any time, the shaft functionality of the motor may be evaluated by completing a motor start study. Consequently, it aids in estimating the allowable torque constraints of the device. 

Studies of motors are starting to identify the machine’s voltage and whether an electric motor can start and accelerate. Skilled personnel at CareLabs conduct motor start studies to thoroughly evaluate your electric motor. We provide motor start analysis services in every major city in Taiwanese, including Taipei, Kaohsiung, Taichung, and Tainan. Reserve your motor starting study now or contact us for a price!

Home /Services /Motor Start Analysis
Motor Start Analysis
Contact Us
LETS DISCOVER MORE

CareLabs provides businesses in Taiwan with a specialized Motor start research and analysis service. The primary purpose of our motor start analysis service is to evaluate the non-typical impacts of starting a large motor. In addition to appropriately connecting the electric motor to the line voltage, there are several other criteria to assess before starting an electric motor, such nuisance tripping, excessive nominal currents, and flickering or fading light, all of which indicate a faulty power supply. CareLabs will assign qualified personnel to your location to collect current and historical motor installation data. Then, using the acquired data and manufacturer input, calculations are performed to forecast future consequences, and recommendations for mitigating the impacts are provided. 

Both static and dynamic simulation approaches may be utilized to evaluate motor starting procedures:

The measured starting impedance of the electric motor is modelled to get the solution for the load flow.
The short-circuit technique is used in pre-fault short circuit circumstances, whereas voltage drop calculations are used for motor starting currents.
The dynamic model of the electric motor may be used to determine the effect if a detailed dynamic model of the electric motor’s circuit and load-torque characteristics is available. of motor starting in a conventional transient stability procedure.
When extensive dynamic model information for an electric motor is not available, transient stability simulations can be employed.
Motor Start Analysis Is Required in Taiwanese:

The use of motors in contemporary industrial systems and the size of electric motors are increasing dramatically. When a large motor linked to a line is started, it can disrupt the electric motor, locally connected loads, and electrically distant buses. The terminal voltage should be at or above 80% of the rated voltage when starting an electric motor. The square of a motor’s terminal voltage is exactly proportional to torque; hence voltage difference affects the load torque of a motor. In the event of a substantial voltage drop, running machines may approach their breaking torque and decelerate or halt. Voltage drops influence, among other things, electrical equipment, sensitive control devices, and lighting loads. In addition, a large initial current reduces terminal voltage, which increases the current taken by all systems supplying a continuous load. Additionally, defective low-voltage relays are identified. 

When Is a Motor Start Analysis Necessary?
Prior to or during the acquisition of a large motor.
If no generator is provided and the motor rating exceeds 30% of the base KVA rating of the supply transformer.
If the generator is the sole source of power for the system and the motor rating exceeds 10-15% of the generator’s KVA rating.
If the generator is the only source of power for the system. If many motors start concurrently in an industrial setting, a motor start study may be essential.
Concerns with voltage loss while starting a motor with a large capacity are exacerbated by the limited capacity of smaller power supply. The examination of motor start-up aids comprehension of the output of systems incorporating minute generators.
Motor Start Benefits Analysis:

Examination of motor launch Utilizing a motor or starting technique with favorable speed-torque characteristics is useful. To avoid exceeding the torque constraints of the equipment, any unexpected loads must be accelerated under supervision. At any time, the shaft functionality of the motor may be evaluated by completing a motor start study. Consequently, it aids in estimating the allowable torque constraints of the device. 

Studies of motors are starting to identify the machine’s voltage and whether an electric motor can start and accelerate. Skilled personnel at CareLabs conduct motor start studies to thoroughly evaluate your electric motor. We provide motor start analysis services in every major city in Taiwanese, including Taipei, Kaohsiung, Taichung, and Tainan. Reserve your motor starting study now or contact us for a price!

Home /Services /Power Quality Analysis

Power Quality
Analysis

Contact Us

What is it?

Power quality analysis checks the power supply for safety and efficiency.

Years of experience?

Electrical Standards

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LETS DISCOVER MORE

CareLabs analyses the quality of power for Taiwan businesses. Our goal is to protect your electrical installations, electrical equipment, and appliances from problems resulting from power supply variations. Many businesses and more individual customers utilize data collection, monitoring, and instrumentation equipment susceptible to power supply fluctuations. Extraordinarily little electrical equipment is constructed to withstand the voltage fluctuation spectrum that is a common characteristic of distributed electricity. CareLabs will help you with power quality issues associated with equipment installation, control, protection, and power conditioning. 

Power quality analysis objective:

  • The assessment of disparities between the electrical system and the load.
  • Evaluating the work environment to improve power quality simulation methodologies.
  • Anticipating the efficacy and performance of load equipment
  • Enhance the power factor and reliability of the equipment.
  • Avoid excessive energy consumption.
  • To avoid failures and disruptions.
  • Reducing power quality anomalies such harmonics, spikes, and transients.
  • Assist in preventing voltage/frequency spikes and PLC lockups.
  • To prevent, among other things, capacitor explosions, transformer overheating, and annoyance tripping.
  • Significantly lower utility costs
  • Enhance the electrical network’s and its components’ longevity.
  • Evaluate electrical equipment’s resilience to variations.

Power Quality Analysis Is Required in Taiwan:

All electrical networks are susceptible to supply interruptions, which occur more often when storms, natural disasters, or accidents damage power wires. Variations in the power supply are very normal. There are three sorts of variations, disturbances, and distortions in the qualities of the electricity transmitted by a network.

1. When a frequency exceeds its typical frequency range, it deviates from that range.

2. Voltage disruptions occur when the voltage form is maintained but the voltage amplitude exceeds the normal range.

2.1 Voltage drops (dips) and voltage spikes lasting up to one minute are examples of short-lasting variations.

2.2 Long duration voltage fluctuations include both under voltages and over voltages that continue longer than one minute.

2.3 Unbalanced voltage – occurs when the load is distributed unevenly across the phases, causing significant heating of equipment.

2.4 Voltage differences between neutral and ground

3.Disturbances caused by voltage distortion, which occurs when the shape of the voltage is not uniform or sinusoidal.

3.1 Device heating, increased losses, inefficient power consumption, and resonance are all caused by harmonics and inter harmonics problems.

3.2 Transients lasting less than a tenth of a second can cause damage to switch contact surfaces, system inefficiency, and premature failure of motor drives and control.

What is Included in a Power Quality Analysis?

Identifying diverse power quality disturbances requires a variety of techniques, including the following:

  • Harmonic Analysis: This technique uses computer algorithms to identify and forecast likely harmonic problems and reduce techniques.
  • This examination will identify surges and transients.
  • This investigation would examine the voltage’s short-term reduction and increase, as well as its value and trajectory.
  • Assessment of Reactive Force This study will identify the preferred reactive power at the distribution and load ends.
  • Analysis of Captive Electricity: This research would quantify and synchronize captive electricity to meet demand while reducing energy surcharges and fuel use.
  • Load Flow Analysis: This analysis predicts the magnitudes of power flow, power factor, voltage levels, and losses in system branches based on the operating parameters supplied.

For the benefit of all of its clients, CareLabs is devoted to providing the finest quality service to improve the quality and dependability of your electrical installation. In all major cities, including Taipei, Kaohsiung, Taichung, and Tainan, we provide power quality assessments. Contact us immediately to assess the quality of your power or to receive a quote! 

RELATED SERVICES

Arc Flash Study Arc flash analyses, also known as arc flash hazard analyses, are the process of looking at electrical systems to identify potential dangers related to arc flash accidents.
Read 01 05 Harmonic Study Harmonic voltage analysis is a type of mathematical method that is used to estimate potential resonances and harmonic distortion levels based on the data that has been collected.


Read 02 05 Power Quality Study Power quality analysis is the process of assessing the electrical power supply to ensure that it satisfies the requirements necessary for the safe and efficient operation of electrical systems.
Read 03 05 Power System Analysis The Power System Study & Analysis service assures the safety, efficacy, and efficiency of your facility's power system in the event that there is an interruption in the supply of electrical power.






Read 04 05 Motor Start Analysis A motor start analysis service may offer insight into the optimal method of starting a motor and the optimal power system design to minimize the impact of the motor starting on the power system.
Read 05 05

Power System Study
and Analysis

Contact Us

What is it?

Power quality analysis checks the power supply for safety and efficiency.

Years of experience?

Terms of service?

Electrical Standards

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LRED

LETS DISCOVER MORE

Electrical safety inspections, electrical system design simulations, power quality analysis studies, and power system study and analysis are just a few of the electrical installation services that CareLabs provides. Our team of electrical professionals conducts load flow and short circuit studies, protective device coordination studies, harmonic emission and filter design studies, transient analysis, and network stability studies using ETAP (Electrical Transient Analysis Program), the most effective tool for power system transient analysis. It allows us to collaborate directly with clients to develop and implement the most efficient power compensation and solutions. 

Why Is a Power System Analysis Important?

Every industrial and commercial facility must have a reliable, dependable, and well-maintained electrical system. Even a little power outage or failure can have devastating consequences, including extended downtime, damage to key equipment, and the expense of restoring it. An ongoing analysis of your facility’s power system may show that it is both reliable and safe in regular and abnormal circumstances. A power system must undergo detailed analysis at each step of design, installation, and operation. 

Using the ETAP software tool, our studies and evaluations of power systems will provide an overview of the system’s performance during normal operation and transient fault conditions. It defines a collection of unique engineering analytical studies used to examine the response of an electric power system to events occurring over various time intervals. 

Numerous studies indicate the state of the electrical system at your facility. Several examples may be included in a research report on power system analysis: 

  • Breaker switches
  • Constructing a security plan
  • Move of the Load
  • Assessments that are always changing
  • Investigating Arc Flash
  • How well does power function?
  • Study on harmonics
  • The engine being started
  • Research in Earth science

It assists businesses in establishing a baseline for other aspects of overall plant performance, such as the electrical safety program. These findings suggest that safe and dependable operations are possible. 

Load Flow Analysis:

Load flow analyses offer the computation of voltages and currents in various network segments, as well as the selection of equipment with the appropriate rating. ETAP’s built-in load flow function may be used to perform load flows, which provide information on the power loading, power factor, voltages, and currents of a power system. A power system must do short-circuit study to determine how fault conditions develop. Manually doing short circuit studies is a time-consuming endeavor, and it is far more efficient to use software like ETAP. To investigate the impacts of three-phase, L-G, L-L, and L-L-G failures in distribution systems, ETAP is used to model system protection devices.  

Short-Term Studies:

Lightning strikes and switching operations generate transient over voltages and currents, which are determined by transient studies. The results define the amount of insulation and surge protection required. 

Transitory Research:

The size and shape of overvoltage and current spikes brought on by lightning strikes and switching processes are assessed in transient research. Based on the outcomes, you may decide how much insulation and surge protection you need.

Investigations into Protection Coordination:

This research is intended to validate the various security devices on your system. It ensures that they are coordinated and proportionate to the equipment they are protecting.

The following essential functions are frequently included in our power system analysis procedure:

  • Data collection technique
  • Data analysis and client meeting
  • Equipment data collecting
  • Single-line diagram model
  • ETAP power system simulation
  • Short circuit and load flow analysis
  • Research on protection coordination
  • Arc flash analysis
  • Overview of the study report
  • Risk assessment and mitigation planning
  • Undertaking administration

Carelabs Included in the Components of Power System Analysis Are the Following:

  • Possibilities for lowering energy prices and demand
  • Reports highlighting problem areas
  • Suggestions for addressing these issues
  • Information on how and where energy is utilized.
  • Evaluation of safety equipment ratings
  • Contribute to the prevention of damage and harm.
  • The effect of harmonics and impulses

Our power system analysis studies focus on analysing the client’s electrical system and providing methods for enhancing its performance and supply security. Cities such as Taipei, Kaohsiung, Taichung, and Tainan are served by our power system analysis and study services. Contact us immediately for a quote or to organize a study and analysis of your power system!

Read 03 05 Power System Analysis The Power System Study & Analysis service assures the safety, efficacy, and efficiency of your facility's power system in the event that there is an interruption in the supply of electrical power.






Read 04 05 Motor Start Analysis A motor start analysis service may offer insight into the optimal method of starting a motor and the optimal power system design to minimize the impact of the motor starting on the power system.
